persons.name: Man
persons.text: Are you going to the concert tonight?

persons.name: Woman
persons.text: I would love to, but I have a huge biology exam tomorrow morning.

context.EN: The woman implies she needs to study instead of going out.
context.ID: Si wanita mengisyaratkan bahwa dia harus belajar alih-alih pergi keluar.

options.text: She will attend the concert later.
She is unable to go to the concert.
She has already taken her biology exam.
She wants the man to help her study.

reason.EN: Her upcoming exam prevents her from attending the entertainment event.
reason.ID: Ujian yang akan datang menghalanginya untuk menghadiri acara hiburan tersebut.

persons.name: Woman
persons.text: Could you help me move this heavy table?

persons.name: Man
persons.text: My hands are tied at the moment.

context.EN: The man uses an idiom to express that he is currently busy or unavailable.
context.ID: Si pria menggunakan idiom untuk menyatakan bahwa dia sedang sibuk saat ini.

options.text: He is wearing gloves.
He cannot assist the woman right now.
He wants to tie the table with a rope.
He will help her move it immediately.

reason.EN: The phrase 'hands are tied' means having no freedom or time to act or help.
reason.ID: Frasa 'hands are tied' berarti tidak memiliki kebebasan atau waktu untuk bertindak atau membantu.

persons.name: Man
persons.text: I'm really sorry I missed your presentation yesterday.

persons.name: Woman
persons.text: Don't worry about it, you didn't miss much anyway.

context.EN: The woman downplays the importance of her presentation to comfort the man.
context.ID: Si wanita meremehkan pentingnya presentasinya untuk menenangkan si pria.

options.text: The presentation was very long.
The man should have attended the event.
It is fine that the man wasn't there.
She forgot to give her presentation.

reason.EN: By saying 'you didn't miss much', she indicates that his absence was not a big deal.
reason.ID: Dengan mengatakan 'you didn't miss much', dia menunjukkan bahwa ketidakhadiran si pria bukan masalah besar.

persons.name: Woman
persons.text: How did you like the new restaurant downtown?

persons.name: Man
persons.text: The food was reasonable, but the service left a lot to be desired.

context.EN: The man uses a polite idiom to express dissatisfaction with the staff's performance.
context.ID: Si pria menggunakan idiom sopan untuk menyatakan ketidakpuasan terhadap kinerja staf.

question.text: What is the man's opinion of the restaurant?

options.text: He liked the service more than the food.
He thought both the food and service were excellent.
He found the service to be unsatisfactory.
He thinks the restaurant is too expensive.

reason.EN: 'Left a lot to be desired' means that something is bad or not good enough.
reason.ID: 'Left a lot to be desired' berarti bahwa sesuatu itu buruk atau tidak cukup baik.

persons.name: Man
persons.text: Do you think Professor Smith will extend the deadline for the essay?

persons.name: Woman
persons.text: Not on your life!

context.EN: The woman uses a strong idiomatic negative expression to reject the possibility.
context.ID: Si wanita menggunakan ekspresi negatif idiomatik yang kuat untuk menolak kemungkinan tersebut.

options.text: The professor will definitely grant an extension.
She thinks the essay is due next week.
The professor is highly unlikely to change the due date.
She has already finished her essay.

reason.EN: The idiom 'not on your life' is used to say that something will definitely not happen.
reason.ID: Idiom 'not on your life' digunakan untuk mengatakan bahwa sesuatu pasti tidak akan terjadi.

persons.name: Woman
persons.text: Should we take the highway to the airport?

persons.name: Man
persons.text: At this time of day, it's usually bumper to bumper.

context.EN: The man describes a traffic condition without using the word 'traffic'.
context.ID: Si pria menggambarkan kondisi lalu lintas tanpa menggunakan kata 'traffic'.

options.text: The highway is the fastest route.
There is a lot of heavy traffic on the highway.
They need to repair their car's bumper.
The airport is close to the highway.

reason.EN: 'Bumper to bumper' is an idiom used to describe a long line of vehicles moving very slowly.
reason.ID: 'Bumper to bumper' adalah idiom yang digunakan untuk menggambarkan antrean panjang kendaraan yang bergerak sangat lambat.

persons.name: Man
persons.text: I can't believe we've been waiting for our food for forty minutes.

persons.name: Woman
persons.text: Let's track down our server and see what's going on.

context.EN: The woman suggests taking action to find out the reason for the delay.
context.ID: Si wanita menyarankan untuk mengambil tindakan guna mencari tahu alasan keterlambatan.

question.text: What does the woman suggest doing?

options.text: Leaving the restaurant immediately.
Waiting for another ten minutes.
Finding the waiter to ask about their order.
Ordering a different dish.

reason.EN: To 'track down' means to find someone or something after searching for them.
reason.ID: Arti dari 'track down' adalah menemukan seseorang atau sesuatu setelah mencarinya.

persons.name: Woman
persons.text: Are you ready to give your speech to the student council?

persons.name: Man
persons.text: I've got butterflies in my stomach, to be honest.

context.EN: The man uses a common idiom to describe his internal emotional state before a public performance.
context.ID: Si pria menggunakan idiom umum untuk menggambarkan keadaan emosional internalnya sebelum tampil di depan umum.

options.text: He feels sick from something he ate.
He is nervous about speaking.
He is excited and happy.
He forgot his speech notes.

reason.EN: Having 'butterflies in one's stomach' is a metaphor for feeling anxious or nervous.
reason.ID: Memiliki 'butterflies in one's stomach' adalah metafora untuk merasa cemas atau gugup.

persons.name: Man
persons.text: The weather forecast says it's going to pour all weekend.

persons.name: Woman
persons.text: There goes our plan for a beach picnic.

context.EN: The woman reacts to the weather report by acknowledging the cancellation of their outdoor activity.
context.ID: Si wanita bereaksi terhadap laporan cuaca dengan mengakui pembatalan aktivitas luar ruangan mereka.

options.text: They should go to the beach anyway.
They will have to cancel the picnic.
She doesn't believe the weather forecast.
They can have a picnic inside the car.

reason.EN: The phrase 'there goes our plan' indicates that a plan cannot happen anymore due to new circumstances.
reason.ID: Frasa 'there goes our plan' menunjukkan bahwa suatu rencana tidak dapat terlaksana lagi karena keadaan baru.

persons.name: Woman
persons.text: Did you manage to get a ticket for the championship game?

persons.name: Man
persons.text: They were sold out by the time I logged online.

context.EN: The man explains his failure to secure admission by mentioning the lack of availability.
context.ID: Si pria menjelaskan kegagalannya mendapatkan tiket masuk dengan menyebutkan ketiadaan ketersediaan.

options.text: He forgot to log online to check the tickets.
He was unable to purchase a ticket.
He bought the ticket at a higher price.
He decided not to go to the game.

reason.EN: Since the tickets were 'sold out', no more items were available for him to buy.
reason.ID: Karena tiketnya 'sold out' (habis terjual), tidak ada lagi barang yang tersedia untuk dia beli.

persons.name: Man
persons.text: I tried calling Mark several times, but he didn't pick up.

persons.name: Woman
persons.text: Why don't you drop him a line instead?

context.EN: The woman suggests an alternative method of communication using an idiomatic verb phrase.
context.ID: Si wanita menyarankan metode komunikasi alternatif menggunakan frasa kerja idiomatik.

options.text: Calling Mark again later.
Writing a message or note to Mark.
Visiting Mark at his house.
Forgetting about communicating with Mark.

reason.EN: To 'drop someone a line' means to send a brief note, letter, or message to them.
reason.ID: 'Drop someone a line' berarti mengirimkan pesan singkat, surat, atau catatan kepada seseorang.

persons.name: Woman
persons.text: I think the prices at this grocery store are getting out of hand.

persons.name: Man
persons.text: Tell me about it! I spent twice as much today as I did last month.

context.EN: The man expresses total agreement with the woman's statement using a common conversational phrase.
context.ID: Si pria menyatakan persetujuan penuh dengan pernyataan si wanita menggunakan frasa percakapan umum.

options.text: He wants the woman to explain the prices to him.
He strongly agrees that the prices are rising high.
He thinks the store has good discounts.
He rarely goes shopping at this grocery store.

reason.EN: The phrase 'Tell me about it!' is used to agree completely with a negative remark someone has made.
reason.ID: Frasa 'Tell me about it!' digunakan untuk sangat setuju dengan keluhan atau komentar negatif orang lain.

persons.name: Man
persons.text: Do you have time to review my research paper today?

persons.name: Woman
persons.text: I'm swamped with grading right now, but I could look at it on Friday.

context.EN: The woman explains her current workload and offers a delayed alternative.
context.ID: Si wanita menjelaskan beban kerjanya saat ini dan menawarkan alternatif waktu yang ditunda.

question.text: What will the woman probably do?

options.text: Read the man's paper immediately.
Refuse to read the paper at all.
Check the man's paper at the end of the week.
Help the man grade some assignments.

reason.EN: She indicates she is too busy ('swamped') now but is available on Friday.
reason.ID: Dia menunjukkan bahwa dia terlalu sibuk ('swamped') sekarang tetapi memiliki waktu luang di hari Jumat.

persons.name: Woman
persons.text: John looks really tired today.

persons.name: Man
persons.text: He's been burning the candle at both ends studying for finals.

context.EN: The man uses a metaphor to explain John's lack of sleep and intense routine.
context.ID: Si pria menggunakan metafora untuk menjelaskan kurang tidur dan rutinitas intens John.

question.text: What does the man say about John?

options.text: He has been working or studying late into the night.
He accidentally caused a fire while studying.
He prefers studying by candlelight.
He has decided to stop preparing for his exams.

reason.EN: The idiom 'burning the candle at both ends' means staying up late and waking up early to work or study hard.
reason.ID: Idiom 'burning the candle at both ends' berarti tidur sangat larut dan bangun sangat pagi demi bekerja atau belajar keras.

persons.name: Man
persons.text: I'm thinking of signing up for the advanced chemistry course.

persons.name: Woman
persons.text: If I were in your shoes, I'd check the prerequisites first.

context.EN: The woman offers advice by placing herself conceptually in the man's situation.
context.ID: Si wanita menawarkan saran dengan menempatkan dirinya secara konseptual dalam situasi si pria.

question.text: What does the woman advise the man to do?

options.text: Buy new shoes for the chemistry lab.
Enroll in the chemistry course immediately.
Find out the required preparation for the class.
Change his major from chemistry to something else.

reason.EN: The phrase 'in your shoes' is used to give advice, and 'prerequisites' refers to required prior classes.
reason.ID: Frasa 'in your shoes' digunakan untuk memberi saran, dan 'prerequisites' merujuk pada syarat kelas sebelumnya.

persons.name: Woman
persons.text: Did you enjoy the guest speaker's presentation?

persons.name: Man
persons.text: I could barely keep my eyes open.

context.EN: The man expresses his physical reaction to the lecture to indicate boredom or extreme fatigue.
context.ID: Si pria menyatakan reaksi fisiknya terhadap ceramah untuk menunjukkan kebosanan atau kelelahan ekstrem.

question.text: What does the man imply about the presentation?

options.text: It was held in a very dark room.
He found it extremely uninteresting.
He had to close his eyes to concentrate.
It was the best speech he had ever heard.

reason.EN: Not being able to keep one's eyes open typically signifies that the subject was tedious or boring.
reason.ID: Tidak mampu membuka mata biasanya menandakan bahwa topik tersebut menjemukan atau membosankan.

persons.name: Man
persons.text: Should we buy the tickets for the theater now?

persons.name: Woman
persons.text: Better safe than sorry. They might sell out quickly.

context.EN: The woman uses a proverb to advocate for caution and prompt action.
context.ID: Si wanita menggunakan pepatah untuk menganjurkan kehati-hatian dan tindakan cepat.

options.text: Waiting to see if prices drop.
Purchasing the tickets immediately.
Finding a safer theater to go to.
Skipping the theater performance.

reason.EN: 'Better safe than sorry' means it is wiser to act immediately to avoid potential disappointment later.
reason.ID: 'Better safe than sorry' berarti lebih bijaksana untuk bertindak segera guna menghindari potensi kekecewaan nanti.

persons.name: Woman
persons.text: I can't seem to figure out how to solve this math problem.

persons.name: Man
persons.text: Why don't we sleep on it and look at it fresh tomorrow?

context.EN: The man uses an idiom suggesting to delay the decision or problem-solving until the next day.
context.ID: Si pria menggunakan idiom yang menyarankan untuk menunda keputusan atau penyelesaian masalah hingga keesokan harinya.

question.text: What does the man suggest doing?

options.text: Going to bed and dealing with the problem later.
Solving the problem while they are in bed.
Asking a math teacher for help tonight.
Giving up on the problem entirely.

reason.EN: To 'sleep on it' means to delay making a decision or finding a solution until the next day.
reason.ID: 'Sleep on it' berarti menunda pengambilan keputusan atau pencarian solusi hingga hari berikutnya.

persons.name: Man
persons.text: Is David coming to the party with us?

persons.name: Woman
persons.text: He said he was feeling under the weather.

context.EN: The woman explains David's absence using an idiomatic expression related to health.
context.ID: Si wanita menjelaskan ketidakhadiran David menggunakan ekspresi idiomatik yang berkaitan dengan kesehatan.

question.text: What does the woman say about David?

options.text: He is checking the outdoor weather forecast.
He does not like large parties.
He is not feeling well physically.
He will arrive later when the rain stops.

reason.EN: The idiom 'under the weather' means slightly unwell, sick, or indisposed.
reason.ID: Idiom 'under the weather' berarti agak kurang sehat atau sakit.

persons.name: Woman
persons.text: I'm nervous about my job interview tomorrow.

persons.name: Man
persons.text: Just put your best foot forward and you'll do fine.

context.EN: The man offers encouragement by advising the woman to showcase her best qualities.
context.ID: Si pria menawarkan dorongan dengan menasihati si wanita untuk menunjukkan kualitas terbaiknya.

question.text: What does the man advise the woman to do?

options.text: Walk carefully into the interview room.
Make a strong, positive effort to impress.
Buy new shoes for the meeting.
Postpone the interview until she feels ready.

reason.EN: To 'put your best foot forward' means to embark on an undertaking with as much effort and ability as possible.
reason.ID: 'Put your best foot forward' berarti memulai suatu usaha dengan usaha dan kemampuan semaksimal mungkin.

persons.name: Man
persons.text: Did you hear that the library is changing its weekend hours?

persons.name: Woman
persons.text: That's news to me.

context.EN: The woman indicates her lack of prior awareness regarding the informational update.
context.ID: Si wanita menunjukkan ketidaktahuannya sebelumnya mengenai pembaruan informasi tersebut.

options.text: She already knew about the schedule change.
She does not care about the library hours.
She had not heard about this information before.
She writes articles for the university news.

reason.EN: The expression 'That's news to me' indicates that the speaker is hearing the information for the first time.
reason.ID: Ekspresi 'That's news to me' menunjukkan bahwa pembicara baru pertama kali mendengar informasi tersebut.

persons.name: Woman
persons.text: I can't believe the professor gave us so much homework over the break.

persons.name: Man
persons.text: You can say that again!

context.EN: The man uses a colloquial response to express strong agreement with the woman's complaint.
context.ID: Si pria menggunakan tanggapan kolokial untuk menyatakan persetujuan kuat atas keluhan si wanita.

options.text: He wants the woman to repeat what she said.
He fully agrees with the woman's opinion.
He thinks the homework is very easy.
He didn't hear the woman clearly.

reason.EN: The phrase 'You can say that again!' is an idiomatic expression meaning 'I completely agree with you'.
reason.ID: Frasa 'You can say that again!' adalah ekspresi idiomatik yang berarti 'Saya sangat setuju denganmu'.

persons.name: Man
persons.text: Are we still meeting at the cafe at five o'clock?

persons.name: Woman
persons.text: Let's change it to six. Something unexpected has come up.

context.EN: The woman modifies the schedule because an unforeseen event requires her attention.
context.ID: Si wanita mengubah jadwal karena suatu kejadian yang tidak terduga memerlukan perhatiannya.

question.text: What does the woman want to do?

options.text: Cancel the meeting at the cafe entirely.
Postpone their meeting by one hour.
Meet at a different location.
Arrive earlier than originally planned.

reason.EN: Moving the time from five to six means delaying or postponing the appointment by an hour.
reason.ID: Mengubah waktu dari jam lima ke jam enam berarti menunda janji temu selama satu jam.

persons.name: Woman
persons.text: Did you finish writing the lab report last night?

persons.name: Man
persons.text: I stayed up late, but I barely scratched the surface.

context.EN: The man uses an idiom to show that despite his efforts, he only completed a small part of the task.
context.ID: Si pria menggunakan idiom untuk menunjukkan bahwa meskipun telah berusaha, dia hanya menyelesaikan sebagian kecil tugas.

question.text: What does the man imply about the lab report?

options.text: He finished it quickly without any trouble.
He has only done a small fraction of the work.
He accidentally damaged the paper document.
He decided to stop working on it completely.

reason.EN: To 'scratch the surface' means to deal with only a small or superficial part of a subject or task.
reason.ID: 'Scratch the surface' berarti hanya menangani sebagian kecil atau bagian luar dari suatu subjek atau tugas.

persons.name: Man
persons.text: The laptop I bought last week keeps crashing.

persons.name: Woman
persons.text: If I were you, I'd take it right back to the store.

context.EN: The woman advises immediate action regarding a defective product purchase.
context.ID: Si wanita menyarankan tindakan segera terkait pembelian produk yang cacat.

question.text: What does the woman think the man should do?

options.text: Try to fix the laptop himself.
Return the laptop to where he purchased it.
Wait for another week to see if it improves.
Buy a different brand of computer online.

reason.EN: The recommendation to 'take it right back' means returning the faulty item to the shop.
reason.ID: Rekomendasi untuk 'take it right back' berarti mengembalikan barang yang rusak tersebut ke toko.

persons.name: Woman
persons.text: I heard you got the internship at the law firm!

persons.name: Man
persons.text: Yeah, but it was a close call. The interview was tough.

context.EN: The man indicates that success was achieved but only by a very narrow margin.
context.ID: Si pria menunjukkan bahwa kesuksesan berhasil diraih tetapi hanya dengan selisih yang sangat tipis.

options.text: He did not receive the internship offer.
He almost didn't get the position.
He missed the interview phone call.
The law firm is located very close to his home.

reason.EN: A 'close call' refers to a situation in which something bad or unsuccessful almost happened.
reason.ID: 'Close call' merujuk pada situasi di mana sesuatu yang buruk atau kegagalan hampir saja terjadi.

persons.name: Man
persons.text: Do you want to go out for dinner or cook at home?

persons.name: Woman
persons.text: Either way is fine by me. It's up to you.

context.EN: The woman expresses neutral preference, passing the decision power to the man.
context.ID: Si wanita menyatakan pilihan netral, menyerahkan kekuasaan pengambilan keputusan kepada si pria.

options.text: She wants to cook a specific meal at home.
She wants the man to make the final choice.
She dislikes eating out at restaurants.
She is too tired to make a decision.

reason.EN: The phrase 'It's up to you' means that the decision is dependent on the other person's preference.
reason.ID: Frasa 'It's up to you' berarti bahwa keputusan bergantung pada pilihan orang lain tersebut.

persons.name: Woman
persons.text: I hope the professor doesn't call on me today. I didn't read the chapter.

persons.name: Man
persons.text: You're in the same boat as most people in the class.

context.EN: The man uses an idiom to indicate that her unpreparedness is shared by others.
context.ID: Si pria menggunakan idiom untuk menunjukkan bahwa ketidaksiapannya juga dialami oleh orang lain.

options.text: Many students are also unprepared for the class.
The class is going on a field trip by boat.
Most students have completed the reading assignment.
He will help the woman read the chapter now.

reason.EN: To be 'in the same boat' means to be in the same difficult or unfortunate situation as others.
reason.ID: 'In the same boat' berarti berada dalam situasi sulit atau tidak beruntung yang sama dengan orang lain.

persons.name: Man
persons.text: Did you see how angry the manager was during the staff meeting?

persons.name: Woman
persons.text: He really lost his cool, didn't he?

context.EN: The woman confirms the manager's emotional outburst using informal figurative phrasing.
context.ID: Si wanita mengonfirmasi luapan emosi manajer menggunakan ungkapan figuratif informal.

question.text: What do the speakers say about the manager?

options.text: He became very cold during the meeting.
He failed to control his temper.
He canceled the rest of the meeting.
He forgot the main topic of discussion.

reason.EN: To 'lose one's cool' means to lose control of one's temper or emotions.
reason.ID: 'Lose one's cool' berarti kehilangan kendali atas amarah atau emosi seseorang.

persons.name: Woman
persons.text: Are you going to buy that textbook brand new?

persons.name: Man
persons.text: I would if it didn't cost an arm and a leg.

context.EN: The man explains his hesitation using an idiom that emphasizes an excessive financial burden.
context.ID: Si pria menjelaskan keraguannya menggunakan idiom yang menekankan beban keuangan yang berlebihan.

options.text: He will purchase the new textbook anyway.
The textbook is extremely expensive.
He needs to find a book about human anatomy.
He cannot find the book anywhere in store.

reason.EN: The idiom 'costs an arm and a leg' is used to describe something that is very high in price.
reason.ID: Idiom 'costs an arm and a leg' digunakan untuk menggambarkan sesuatu yang harganya sangat mahal.

persons.name: Man
persons.text: Could you help me edit this video before the submission deadline?

persons.name: Woman
persons.text: I'd love to, but I'm completely tied up until next week.

context.EN: The woman rejects the request by expressing her lack of free time using a metaphor.
context.ID: Si wanita menolak permintaan tersebut dengan menyatakan kurangnya waktu luang menggunakan metafora.

options.text: She will assist him with the editing right away.
She is too busy to help him at the moment.
She needs a rope to tie something up.
She doesn't know how to edit videos.

reason.EN: To be 'tied up' means to be occupied, busy, or unavailable for other activities.
reason.ID: Menjadi 'tied up' berarti sedang sibuk, terikat pekerjaan, atau tidak tersedia untuk aktivitas lain.

persons.name: Woman
persons.text: The printer in the student lounge is out of order again.

persons.name: Man
persons.text: Not surprising. It's been on its last legs for months.

context.EN: The man remarks on the machinery's old age and near-failure state.
context.ID: Si pria mengomentari usia tua mesin dan kondisi yang hampir rusak total.

question.text: What does the man imply about the printer?

options.text: It was recently repaired by the staff.
It is a brand-new model with legs.
It is old and near the end of its usefulness.
It runs faster than other printers.

reason.EN: The phrase 'on its last legs' means near the end of life, usefulness, or existence.
reason.ID: Frasa 'on its last legs' berarti hampir mati, tidak dapat digunakan lagi, atau rusak karena tua.

persons.name: Man
persons.text: I can't believe how quickly the seminar passed.

persons.name: Woman
persons.text: Time flies when you're learning something interesting.

context.EN: The woman relies on a proverb regarding the perception of temporal passage during engaging tasks.
context.ID: Si wanita bersandar pada pepatah mengenai persepsi berjalannya waktu selama tugas yang menarik.

options.text: The seminar felt short because it was engaging.
They arrived late to the presentation room.
The speaker talked way too fast.
She wants to leave the class early.

reason.EN: The proverb 'time flies' signifies that time seems to move very rapidly when one is enjoying themselves.
reason.ID: Pepatah 'time flies' menandakan bahwa waktu terasa berlalu sangat cepat ketika seseorang sedang menikmati situasi.

persons.name: Woman
persons.text: Should we inform Professor Davis about our project delay?

persons.name: Man
persons.text: We might as well get it over with.

context.EN: The man suggests facing an unpleasant task promptly rather than avoiding it.
context.ID: Si pria menyarankan untuk segera menghadapi tugas yang tidak menyenangkan alih-alih menghindarinya.

options.text: Delaying the notification as long as possible.
Telling the professor about the issue immediately.
Canceling the project without telling anyone.
Asking another teacher for a second opinion.

reason.EN: To 'get it over with' means to do something unpleasant or difficult immediately so that you do not have to worry about it anymore.
reason.ID: 'Get it over with' berarti melakukan sesuatu yang tidak menyenangkan atau sulit dengan segera agar tidak perlu mengkhawatirkannya lagi.

persons.name: Man
persons.text: Mark looks very stressed about the upcoming debate competition.

persons.name: Woman
persons.text: He just needs to keep his chin up.

context.EN: The woman offers an encouraging idiom aimed at boosting someone's morale.
context.ID: Si wanita menawarkan idiom penyemangat yang bertujuan untuk meningkatkan moral seseorang.

options.text: Mark should look up while speaking.
Mark should remain hopeful and cheerful.
Mark needs to practice his posture.
Mark will likely lose the debate.

reason.EN: To 'keep your chin up' means to stay positive and optimistic in a difficult emotional situation.
reason.ID: 'Keep your chin up' berarti tetap positif dan optimis dalam situasi emosional yang sulit.

persons.name: Woman
persons.text: I tried to solve this coding error, but I'm stuck.

persons.name: Man
persons.text: Let me take a look. Two heads are better than one.

context.EN: The man applies a proverbial concept emphasizing the benefit of cooperative problem-solving.
context.ID: Si pria menerapkan konsep pepatah yang menekankan manfaat dari pemecahan masalah secara kooperatif.

options.text: He thinks the error is too complex for them.
He believes working together will help find a solution.
He wants to work on the code by himself.
He thinks the woman should find another partner.

reason.EN: The phrase 'two heads are better than one' implies that two people working together can solve a problem more easily than one.
reason.ID: Frasa 'two heads are better than one' menyiratkan bahwa dua orang yang bekerja bersama dapat menyelesaikan masalah lebih mudah daripada satu orang.

persons.name: Man
persons.text: Are you going to register for the photography workshop?

persons.name: Woman
persons.text: I'm still on the fence about it.

context.EN: The woman describes her undecided mental state using a classic physical metaphor.
context.ID: Si wanita menggambarkan kondisi mentalnya yang belum memutuskan menggunakan metafora fisik klasik.

options.text: She has already signed up for the workshop.
She is unable to attend due to a fence.
She has not made a definitive choice yet.
She thinks the workshop is a bad idea.

reason.EN: To be 'on the fence' means to be undecided or uncommitted regarding two options.
reason.ID: Menjadi 'on the fence' berarti belum memutuskan atau belum menentukan pilihan di antara dua opsi.

persons.name: Woman
persons.text: Did the group select a topic for the history presentation?

persons.name: Man
persons.text: We finally hit upon an idea after hours of discussion.

context.EN: The man details the outcome of their group meeting using a phrasal verb of discovery.
context.ID: Si pria merinci hasil pertemuan kelompok mereka menggunakan kata kerja frasa tentang penemuan.

options.text: They physical struck something during the class.
They managed to discover a suitable topic.
They decided to cancel the presentation.
They disagreed on every single idea.

reason.EN: To 'hit upon' something means to think of or discover a solution or idea by chance or effort.
reason.ID: 'Hit upon' sesuatu berarti memikirkan atau menemukan solusi atau ide melalui peluang atau usaha.

persons.name: Man
persons.text: The new assignment rules seem extremely complicated.

persons.name: Woman
persons.text: Don't worry, once you start, it's a piece of cake.

context.EN: The woman comforts the man by evaluating the task's true difficulty level using food imagery.
context.ID: Si wanita menenangkan si pria dengan mengevaluasi tingkat kesulitan sebenarnya dari tugas tersebut menggunakan citra makanan.

question.text: What does the woman think about the assignment?

options.text: It involves cooking or baking food.
It is much easier than it appears.
It will take a long time to complete.
It requires buying a dessert for class.

reason.EN: The idiom 'a piece of cake' is used to describe something that is extraordinarily easy to do.
reason.ID: Idiom 'a piece of cake' digunakan untuk menggambarkan sesuatu yang sangat mudah dilakukan.

persons.name: Woman
persons.text: Did you manage to fix the leak in the bathroom?

persons.name: Man
persons.text: I tried, but it's completely beyond my depth.

context.EN: The man confesses his lack of skill or knowledge for a specific maintenance task.
context.ID: Si pria mengakui kurangnya keterampilan atau pengetahuan untuk tugas pemeliharaan tertentu.

options.text: The bathroom floor is covered in deep water.
He successfully repaired the broken pipe.
He lacks the expertise required to fix it.
He needs to measure the depth of the room.

reason.EN: To be 'beyond one's depth' means to be in a situation that is too difficult or outside of one's capabilities.
reason.ID: Menjadi 'beyond one's depth' berarti berada dalam situasi yang terlalu sulit atau di luar kemampuan seseorang.

persons.name: Man
persons.text: I missed the deadline for the study abroad program.

persons.name: Woman
persons.text: That's a pity, but there's no use crying over spilled milk.

context.EN: The woman advises against useless regret regarding past unchangeable occurrences.
context.ID: Si wanita menasihati agar tidak menyesali hal yang sia-sia terkait peristiwa masa lalu yang tidak dapat diubah.

options.text: The man should clean up the liquid error.
Regretting the past will not fix the current situation.
She thinks he should try to apply again today.
The deadline was not actually important.

reason.EN: The proverb 'no use crying over spilled milk' means it's pointless to worry about past mistakes that cannot be undone.
reason.ID: Pepatah 'no use crying over spilled milk' berarti tidak ada gunanya mengkhawatirkan kesalahan masa lalu yang tidak bisa diubah.

persons.name: Woman
persons.text: Can you believe Arthur won the research grant?

persons.name: Man
persons.text: It came completely out of the blue!

context.EN: The man highlights the unexpected nature of the event using an idiomatic color reference.
context.ID: Si pria menyoroti sifat tak terduga dari peristiwa tersebut menggunakan referensi warna idiomatik.

question.text: What does the man imply about Arthur's grant?

options.text: It was expected by everyone in the department.
It was a total surprise.
It was announced on a clear day.
The grant amount was lower than anticipated.

reason.EN: The idiom 'out of the blue' means completely unexpected, sudden, or without warning.
reason.ID: Idiom 'out of the blue' berarti benar-benar tidak terduga, tiba-tiba, atau tanpa peringatan.

persons.name: Man
persons.text: Are you almost ready to leave for the presentation?

persons.name: Woman
persons.text: Hold your horses, I can't find my car keys.

context.EN: The woman tells the man to reduce his rushing using an old idiomatic command.
context.ID: Si wanita menyuruh si pria untuk mengurangi ketergesa-gesaannya menggunakan perintah idiomatik lama.

options.text: She wants the man to be patient and wait.
She owns multiple animals at home.
She is ready to go to the car now.
She thinks they should walk instead of drive.

reason.EN: The idiomatic expression 'hold your horses' is used to tell someone to stop, slow down, or be patient.
reason.ID: Ekspresi idiomatik 'hold your horses' digunakan untuk memberitahu seseorang agar berhenti, memperlambat, atau bersabar.

persons.name: Woman
persons.text: I can't seem to make ends meet with my current part-time job salary.

persons.name: Man
persons.text: Maybe it's time to look for a full-time position.

context.EN: The woman outlines a financial distress situation without stating literal monetary terms.
context.ID: Si wanita menguraikan situasi kesulitan keuangan tanpa menyatakan istilah moneter secara harfiah.

question.text: What problem is the woman facing?

options.text: She doesn't like her current job tasks.
She is unable to cover her living expenses.
She cannot find the end of a physical rope.
She works too many hours every week.

reason.EN: To 'make ends meet' means to earn enough money to live on or pay for one's necessary expenses.
reason.ID: 'Make ends meet' berarti menghasilkan cukup uang untuk hidup atau membayar biaya-biaya penting seseorang.

persons.name: Man
persons.text: I don't think we should change our project strategy now.

persons.name: Woman
persons.text: Let's play it by ear and see how things develop.

context.EN: The woman favors flexibility over rigid preset planning using a musical idiom.
context.ID: Si wanita lebih menyukai fleksibilitas daripada perencanaan kaku yang ditetapkan sebelumnya menggunakan idiom musik.

options.text: Listening to music while they work.
Dealing with the situation as it happens without strict plans.
Writing a detailed, permanent strategy manual.
Asking the manager for hearing protection.

reason.EN: To 'play it by ear' means to decide instructions or actions as a situation progresses rather than following a firm plan.
reason.ID: 'Play it by ear' berarti memutuskan tindakan seiring berkembangnya situasi daripada mengikuti rencana kaku.

persons.name: Woman
persons.text: How did you manage to finish that thick book in one day?

persons.name: Man
persons.text: I skipped the introduction and got straight to the point.

context.EN: The man explains his reading efficiency technique by focusing only on core substance.
context.ID: Si pria menjelaskan teknik efisiensi membacanya dengan hanya fokus pada substansi inti.

options.text: He read every page very slowly.
He ignored the starting parts and read the main content.
He decided the book was too boring to read.
He lost the book after reading the first page.

reason.EN: Getting 'straight to the point' means addressing the most important or core part directly without wasting time.
reason.ID: Mencapai 'straight to the point' berarti menuju bagian paling penting atau inti secara langsung tanpa membuang waktu.

persons.name: Man
persons.text: Did you tell Sarah about the secret party?

persons.name: Woman
persons.text: I didn't mean to, but I accidentally let the cat out of the bag.

context.EN: The woman admits to revealing hidden information through an accidental verbal slip.
context.ID: Si wanita mengaku telah mengungkapkan informasi tersembunyi melalui kesalahan bicara yang tidak disengaja.

options.text: She brought a pet animal to the party room.
She unintentionally revealed the secret.
She forgot to invite Sarah to the event.
She lost her bag while traveling.

reason.EN: The idiom 'let the cat out of the bag' means to reveal a secret, usually carelessly or by mistake.
reason.ID: Idiom 'let the cat out of the bag' berarti mengungkapkan rahasia, biasanya secara tidak sengaja atau karena kecerobohan.

persons.name: Woman
persons.text: Are you going to try out for the basketball varsity team?

persons.name: Man
persons.text: I think it's a long shot, but I'll give it a try.

context.EN: The man assesses his low probability of success while maintaining an intent to attempt.
context.ID: Si pria menilai probabilitas keberhasilannya yang rendah sambil tetap mempertahankan niat untuk mencoba.

question.text: What does the man think about his chances?

options.text: He is certain he will make the team.
He thinks success is highly unlikely but possible.
He can throw the ball from a long distance.
He has decided to skip the tryouts.

reason.EN: A 'long shot' is an attempt or guess that has only a very small chance of succeeding.
reason.ID: 'Long shot' adalah upaya atau tebakan yang hanya memiliki peluang sangat kecil untuk berhasil.

persons.name: Man
persons.text: Why are you staying late at the lab tonight?

persons.name: Woman
persons.text: I need to catch up on my chemistry data entries.

context.EN: The woman explains her necessity to compensate for accumulated behind-schedule work.
context.ID: Si wanita menjelaskan keharusannya untuk mengompensasi akumulasi pekerjaan yang tertinggal.

options.text: Starting a brand-new experimental study.
Doing overdue work to reach the expected level.
Cleaning the chemistry lab equipment.
Teaching a chemistry class late at night.

reason.EN: To 'catch up' means to do tasks necessary to reach a standard or point from which one is behind.
reason.ID: 'Catch up' berarti melakukan tugas yang diperlukan untuk mencapai standar atau titik di mana seseorang tertinggal.

persons.name: Woman
persons.text: The manager rejected your marketing proposal again.

persons.name: Man
persons.text: Well, back to the drawing board, I guess.

context.EN: The man acknowledges the failure and the requirement to restart the planning process entirely.
context.ID: Si pria mengakui kegagalan dan keharusan untuk memulai kembali proses perencanaan sepenuhnya.

question.text: What will the man probably do next?

options.text: Quit his job at the marketing firm.
Start designing a completely new proposal.
Buy a new drawing board for the office.
Submit the exact same proposal again tomorrow.

reason.EN: The idiom 'back to the drawing board' means to start planning something over again because a previous attempt failed.
reason.ID: Idiom 'back to the drawing board' berarti mulai merencanakan sesuatu dari awal lagi karena upaya sebelumnya gagal.
